landing.title: Nigeria's first open, document-first election results platform.
landing.lede: Built on Form EC8A. Powered by civil society. Accountable to the public. Every result on OpenBallot is anchored to the signed physical document, cross-verified across parties and observers, and recorded on a tamper-evident audit chain.
landing.pillars_lede: Each pillar is independently testable, independently verifiable, and independently catches different failure modes.
landing.feature1_body: Results emerge only when independent parties and observers agree. No single point of failure, no single point of fraud.
landing.feature1_example: Example: when one party agent reports 142 votes and another reports 89 for the same PU, the unit shows orange on the map until reconciliation.
landing.feature2_body: Every figure published on OpenBallot is shown alongside the signed EC8A it was extracted from. You never see a number without the document behind it.
landing.feature2_example: Example: clicking any green polling unit reveals the signed photograph the consensus is anchored to, with its SHA-256 hash.
landing.feature3_body: Every image is SHA-256 hashed at the agent's device, every database event is chained, and Merkle roots are anchored to Ethereum mainnet every 30 minutes.
landing.feature3_example: Example: an auditor downloads the published manifest five years from now and re-verifies every image bit-for-bit.
landing.how_lede: Five steps from a polling unit on election day to a verifiable result on the public map.
landing.how_step1_body: Party agents and accredited observers use the OpenBallot PWA — no app store, no installation — to photograph the signed form. GPS coordinates and SHA-256 are computed on-device.
landing.how_step2_body: The PWA requests a short-lived presigned URL and uploads directly to our object storage. The image's hash is bound at upload time; the bytes cannot be substituted.
landing.how_step3_body: Document AI extracts the structured fields; GPT-4o Vision verifies any field below the confidence floor. Low-confidence extractions land in the consortium review queue, not on the public map.
landing.how_step4_body: Across all submissions for a unit — party agents, observers, INEC IReV — the verification engine returns one of six map states. Disagreement surfaces, never hides.
landing.how_step5_body: Every event is chained into the SHA-256 audit log. Merkle roots are anchored to Ethereum mainnet. After the election, the full evidentiary dataset is released under Creative Commons.
landing.audience_journalist_body: Cite a polling unit by URL. Download the full results CSV. Diff today's INEC upload against the cross-source consensus.
landing.audience_party_body: Upload your agent roster as CSV; dispatch OTPs to every agent; track per-PU submission status during election day.
landing.audience_observer_body: Register your INEC accreditation; on approval, submit EC8As for any polling unit you cover. Domestic and international observer organisations welcome.
landing.audience_researcher_body: Stream the full results CSV (one row per PU × candidate). Download the SHA-256 hash manifest. Verify the audit chain end-to-end in any language.
landing.footer_licence: © 2026 OpenBallot Consortium · Licensed AGPL-3.0 · The form is the truth. The truth is public.
